items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The council held a study session on the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major city infrastructure and development projects planned for the coming year. Residents interested in upcoming construction, road work, or facility improvements should review what projects the city is prioritizing.

items.headline: New tenant protections ordinance introduced for Sunnyvale renters
items.summary: City Council introduced changes to Chapter 19.71 of the Municipal Code to strengthen the Residential Tenant Protections Program. Details of the specific protections being added were not fully outlined in the agenda, but this signals potential new renter safeguards coming to Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Library facilities and customer experience plan moves forward
items.summary: The City Council reviewed a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, 2026. This plan will shape how the city's library system evolves to better serve residents.

items.headline: Downtown building gains bonus square footage for historic upgrades
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street is being elevated from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, allowing the owner to add 5,000 square feet of bonus floor area in exchange for historic and seismic rehabilitation work. This incentive program encourages preservation while allowing downtown development.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A rezoning and redevelopment proposal would convert service commercial land into an 8-story building with 167 residential units (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space. This represents significant new housing near San Antonio Road and will require City Council approval after the quasi-judicial hearing.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8, 2026 with no new hearing date set yet. Residents interested in this project should watch for future notices when the hearing is rescheduled.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda document does not specify which project this contract covers. Residents should monitor future meeting minutes for details on what infrastructure or development work this represents.

items.headline: Grant awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming and arts education in the community.
